speaker: KING
text: I like him not, nor stands it safe with us

speaker: KING
text: To let his madness range. Therefore prepare you.

speaker: KING
text: I your commission will forthwith dispatch,

speaker: KING
text: And he to England shall along with you.

speaker: KING
text: The terms of our estate may not endure

speaker: KING
text: Hazard so near ’s as doth hourly grow

speaker: KING
text: Out of his brows.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: We will ourselves provide.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: Most holy and religious fear it is

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: To keep those many many bodies safe

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: That live and feed upon your Majesty.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: The single and peculiar life is bound

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: With all the strength and armor of the mind

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: To keep itself from noyance, but much more

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: That spirit upon whose weal depends and rests

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: The lives of many. The cess of majesty

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Dies not alone, but like a gulf doth draw

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: What’s near it with it; or it is a massy wheel

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Fixed on the summit of the highest mount,

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: To whose huge spokes ten thousand lesser things

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Are mortised and adjoined, which, when it falls,

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Each small annexment, petty consequence,

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Attends the boist’rous ruin. Never alone

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Did the king sigh, but with a general groan.

speaker: KING
text: Arm you, I pray you, to this speedy voyage,

speaker: KING
text: For we will fetters put about this fear,

speaker: KING
text: Which now goes too free-footed.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: We will haste us.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: My lord, he’s going to his mother’s closet.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Behind the arras I’ll convey myself

speaker: POLONIUS
text: To hear the process. I’ll warrant she’ll tax him home;

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And, as you said (and wisely was it said),

speaker: POLONIUS
text: ’Tis meet that some more audience than a mother,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Since nature makes them partial, should o’erhear

speaker: POLONIUS
text: The speech of vantage. Fare you well, my liege.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: I’ll call upon you ere you go to bed

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And tell you what I know.

speaker: KING
text: Thanks, dear my lord.

speaker: KING
text: O, my offense is rank, it smells to heaven;

speaker: KING
text: It hath the primal eldest curse upon ’t,

speaker: KING
text: A brother’s murder. Pray can I not,

speaker: KING
text: Though inclination be as sharp as will.

speaker: KING
text: My stronger guilt defeats my strong intent,

speaker: KING
text: And, like a man to double business bound,

speaker: KING
text: I stand in pause where I shall first begin

speaker: KING
text: And both neglect. What if this cursèd hand

speaker: KING
text: Were thicker than itself with brother’s blood?

speaker: KING
text: Is there not rain enough in the sweet heavens

speaker: KING
text: To wash it white as snow? Whereto serves mercy

speaker: KING
text: But to confront the visage of offense?

speaker: KING
text: And what’s in prayer but this twofold force,

speaker: KING
text: To be forestallèd ere we come to fall,

speaker: KING
text: Or pardoned being down? Then I’ll look up.

speaker: KING
text: My fault is past. But, O, what form of prayer

speaker: KING
text: Can serve my turn? “Forgive me my foul murder”?

speaker: KING
text: That cannot be, since I am still possessed

speaker: KING
text: Of those effects for which I did the murder:

speaker: KING
text: My crown, mine own ambition, and my queen.

speaker: KING
text: May one be pardoned and retain th’ offense?

speaker: KING
text: In the corrupted currents of this world,

speaker: KING
text: Offense’s gilded hand may shove by justice,

speaker: KING
text: And oft ’tis seen the wicked prize itself

speaker: KING
text: Buys out the law. But ’tis not so above:

speaker: KING
text: There is no shuffling; there the action lies

speaker: KING
text: In his true nature, and we ourselves compelled,

speaker: KING
text: Even to the teeth and forehead of our faults,

speaker: KING
text: To give in evidence. What then? What rests?

speaker: KING
text: Try what repentance can. What can it not?

speaker: KING
text: Yet what can it, when one cannot repent?

speaker: KING
text: O wretched state! O bosom black as death!

speaker: KING
text: O limèd soul, that, struggling to be free,

speaker: KING
text: Art more engaged! Help, angels! Make assay.

speaker: KING
text: Bow, stubborn knees, and heart with strings of steel

speaker: KING
text: Be soft as sinews of the newborn babe.

speaker: KING
text: All may be well.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Now might I do it pat, now he is a-praying,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And now I’ll do ’t.

speaker: HAMLET
text: And so he goes to heaven,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And so am I revenged. That would be scanned:

speaker: HAMLET
text: A villain kills my father, and for that,

speaker: HAMLET
text: I, his sole son, do this same villain send

speaker: HAMLET
text: To heaven.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Why, this is hire and salary, not revenge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: He took my father grossly, full of bread,

speaker: HAMLET
text: With all his crimes broad blown, as flush as May;

speaker: HAMLET
text: And how his audit stands who knows save heaven.

speaker: HAMLET
text: But in our circumstance and course of thought

speaker: HAMLET
text: ’Tis heavy with him. And am I then revenged

speaker: HAMLET
text: To take him in the purging of his soul,

speaker: HAMLET
text: When he is fit and seasoned for his passage?

speaker: HAMLET
text: No.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Up sword, and know thou a more horrid hent.

speaker: HAMLET
text: When he is drunk asleep, or in his rage,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Or in th’ incestuous pleasure of his bed,

speaker: HAMLET
text: At game, a-swearing, or about some act

speaker: HAMLET
text: That has no relish of salvation in ’t—

speaker: HAMLET
text: Then trip him, that his heels may kick at heaven,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And that his soul may be as damned and black

speaker: HAMLET
text: As hell, whereto it goes. My mother stays.

speaker: HAMLET
text: This physic but prolongs thy sickly days.

speaker: KING
text: My words fly up, my thoughts remain below;

speaker: KING
text: Words without thoughts never to heaven go.
